items.title: FDA, Moderna appear aligned on mRNA flu shot heading into adcomm
items.snippet: The Vaccines and Related Biological Products Advisory Committee will meet June 18 to discuss Moderna’s seasonal flu vaccine mRNA-1010 after the FDA initially refused to accept the application in February.

items.title: Neumora, enGene cut staff in cost-saving initiatives
items.snippet: Follow along as BioSpace tracks job cuts and restructuring initiatives.

items.title: Mirum, Incyte bolster case for approval in rare bone disease despite mid-stage miss
items.snippet: While falling short of statistical significance, Incyte and Mirum Pharmaceuticals’ ALK2 inhibitor showed a “clear benefit” in reducing abnormal bone formation in a Phase 2 study of fibrodysplasia ossificans progressiva.

items.title: Alto's depression candidate bolstered by independent study showing mood benefits
items.snippet: Alto Neuroscience is advancing a depression drug based on the dopamine agonist pramipexole, which an independent study has found to help boost feelings of pleasure in patients with mood disorders.

items.title: Intellia’s gene editor ‘keeps pace’ with Ionis in hereditary angioedema
items.snippet: With a one-time dosing profile, Intellia Therapeutics’ gene editing asset could be “paradigm-shifting” for hereditary angioedema, according to Jefferies. The biotech anticipates market approval next year.

items.title: 6 times when drug development got personal
items.snippet: While biopharma’s overarching mission is to develop innovative medicines to improve patient outcomes, for these six people, the motivation came from much closer to home.

items.title: Neumora discards depression asset, lays off staffers after pair of late-stage flops
items.snippet: Neumora Therapeutics is laying off 35% of workers after its most advanced asset failed a pair of Phase 3 studies, sending the biotech’s stock spiraling early Monday.

items.title: Germany rethinking drug price reforms after Lilly, Boehringer withdraw investments: Reuters
items.snippet: Weeks after Boehringer Ingelheim and Eli Lilly retracted billions of dollar in German commitments, the nation’s government is reportedly changing a contentious element of its planned healthcare reforms.

items.title: Elicio crashes on midstage pancreatic cancer miss but will advance to Phase 3
items.snippet: Elicio Therapeutics’ investigational cancer immunotherapy failed to meet the primary endpoint of disease-free survival in a Phase 2 trial—a result the company attributed mostly to a disproportionate number of patients with higher residual disease.

items.title: Sanofi wins type 1 diabetes nod for Tzield after requesting to revoke CNPV
items.snippet: Sanofi makes no mention of the Commissioner’s National Priority Voucher. Tzield was awarded the ticket in October 2025, but Sanofi requested withdrawal from the program after former CDER head Tracy Beth Høeg reportedly expressed skepticism of the drug.

items.title: Rhythm’s obesity drug scores ‘better than expected’ weight loss in rare genetic disease
items.snippet: Rhythm Pharmaceuticals’ Imcivree reduced fat—while boosting muscle—in patients with Prader-Willi syndrome.

items.title: Lilly sees quick return on $2.3B Ajax buy as blood cancer drug shows early efficacy
items.snippet: Eli Lilly’s new JAK2 inhibitor—which it obtained from the recent acquisition of Ajax Therapeutics—reduced spleen volume by more than a third in 70% of patients with myelofibrosis.

items.title: The commercial launch playbook: Team structure, readiness and execution
items.snippet: Intellia Therapeutics's Senior Vice President Maria Natale discusses why the most successful launches are shaped long before approval, with strategy, structure and patient insight at the core.

items.title: Despite FDA trial clearance, ibogaine metabolite won’t receive red carpet treatment
items.snippet: DemeRx is launching the first U.S. clinical trial of an ibogaine-derived drug candidate, marking a pivotal moment for a controversial psychedelic long sidelined by safety concerns.

items.title: Consummate chameleon Amylyx nears regulatory run in obesity surgery complication
items.snippet: After suffering the market withdrawal of its only product, Amylyx is gearing up for a pivotal Phase 3 readout in post-bariatric hypoglycemia. But the company's driving ethos is still to treat “debilitating, devastating” neurodegenerative diseases, co-CEO Justin Klee told BioSpace.

items.title: How does ibogaine work? Understanding matrix logic to make a safer drug
items.snippet: Ibogaine’s unconventional “matrix pharmacology” may underlie both its therapeutic promise and unpredictable cardiac risks. Unraveling this mechanism could help drug developers hoping to bring ibogaine analogs to market.

items.title: FDA imposes import alert on Indian plant after inspectors flag GMP failings
items.snippet: Officials sanctioned Dabur India months after FDA inspectors found bird droppings and data integrity deficiencies during an inspection of the plant.

items.title: Amgen shores up Tavneos’ FDA defense with Duke data analysis
items.snippet: After the FDA flagged patient deaths linked to Amgen's rare disease drug Tavneos and called for its voluntary removal, the pharma recruited an independent data analysis from Duke researchers to help build the case for the drug's continued market approval.

items.title: Novartis’ $12B Avidity buy pays dividends with Phase 1/2 muscular dystrophy win
items.snippet: The RNA-based medicine is one of a handful of antibody-oligonucleotide conjugates that Novartis acquired last October when it took over neuromuscular-focused Avidity Biosciences.

items.title: Novo hit with cybersecurity breach, urges vigilance among trial participants
items.snippet: Novo Nordisk said that the information affected by the breach shouldn’t allow third parties to “identify participants in our clinical trials” despite “unauthorized access” to patients’ personal data.

items.title: Lilly just bought renowned regulator Peter Marks a pipeline to play with
items.snippet: FDA veteran Peter Marks will now shape the future of Eli Lilly’s vaccines work after the buys of Curevo, LimmaTech Biologics and Vaccine Company for up to $3.8 billion total.

items.title: 3 top FDA, CDC roles filled by 15 different people in less than 18 months
items.snippet: The Department of Health and Human Services is spinning its wheels, unable to establish steady leadership at three major divisions—the CDC and the FDA’s two primary review units.

items.title: Unforced errors flummox HHS’ radical transparency bid—again
items.snippet: Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s health department has consistently touted radical transparency as being key to its mission. Recent instances—the FDA’s decision not to disclose the recipients of three Commissioner’s National Priority Vouchers and FDA and CDC choices not to publish vaccine-related papers—call this intent into q...

items.title: BioSpace elevates journalism as biopharma evolves in the Trump era
items.snippet: Neal and Azbee awards have validated our approach to reporting on the industry at a time of unprecedented shifts at the FDA and other federal agencies.
